Enabled.Name: Enable Counterspell workflow
Enabled.Hint: Intercept recognized Counterspell spells and use the Counterspell PLUS workflow.

Ruleset.Name: Counterspell rules
Ruleset.Hint: Choose the rule used to resolve Counterspell.

CounterspellSpecialMinimum.Name: Counterspell: Special Spellcaster minimum d20
CounterspellSpecialMinimum.Hint: Minimum kept d20 result when the GM marks the Counterspell caster as a Special Spellcaster. Range: 1–20. Default: 15.

DispelEnabled.Name: Enable Dispel Magic workflow
DispelEnabled.Hint: Intercept recognized Dispel Magic spells and use the Counterspell PLUS workflow.

DispelRuleset.Name: Dispel Magic rules
DispelRuleset.Hint: Choose the rule used to resolve Dispel Magic independently from Counterspell.

DispelSpecialMinimum.Name: Dispel Magic: Special Spellcaster minimum d20
DispelSpecialMinimum.Hint: Minimum kept d20 result when the GM marks the Dispel Magic caster as a Special Spellcaster. Range: 1–20. Default: 15.

DispelDefenseBase.Name: Dispel Magic: normal spell defense base
DispelDefenseBase.Hint: Homebrew base for an ordinary spell effect. Default: 10. Any numeric value is allowed.

DispelScrollDefenseBase.Name: Dispel Magic: spell scroll defense base
DispelScrollDefenseBase.Hint: Independent homebrew base for an effect cast from a scroll. Default: 7. Any numeric value is allowed.

DispelGlyphDefenseBase.Name: Dispel Magic: Spell Glyph defense base
DispelGlyphDefenseBase.Hint: Independent homebrew base for an effect cast from a Glyph. Default: 10. Any numeric value is allowed.

ScrollDefenseBase.Name: Counterspell: spell scroll defense base
ScrollDefenseBase.Hint: Counterspell homebrew base added to the stored spell level and creator values for a scroll. Default: 7. Any numeric value is allowed.

GlyphDefenseBase.Name: Counterspell: Spell Glyph defense base
GlyphDefenseBase.Hint: Counterspell homebrew base added to the stored spell level and creator values for a Glyph. Default: 10. Any numeric value is allowed.

Names.Name: Recognized Counterspell names
Names.Hint: Comma-separated spell names. Parenthetical suffixes such as Counterspell (Alternate) are recognized automatically.

DispelNames.Name: Recognized Dispel Magic names
DispelNames.Hint: Comma-separated spell names. Parenthetical suffixes are recognized automatically.

WildMagic.Name: Wild Magic message
WildMagic.Hint: Post a Wild Magic message when Counterspell targets another Counterspell.

RemoveCurseEnabled.Name: Enable Remove Curse workflow
RemoveCurseEnabled.Hint: Intercept recognized Remove Curse spells and use the Counterspell PLUS workflow.

RemoveCurseRuleset.Name: Remove Curse rules
RemoveCurseRuleset.Hint: Choose the rule used to resolve Remove Curse independently from Counterspell and Dispel Magic.
RemoveCurseRuleset.Homebrew: Homebrew: roll against passive curse defense

RemoveCurseSpecialMinimum.Name: Remove Curse: Special Spellcaster minimum d20
RemoveCurseSpecialMinimum.Hint: Minimum kept d20 result when the GM marks the Remove Curse caster as a Special Spellcaster. Range: 1–20. Default: 15.

RemoveCurseDefenseBase.Name: Remove Curse: normal curse defense base
RemoveCurseDefenseBase.Hint: Homebrew base for an ordinary curse effect. Default: 10. Any numeric value is allowed.

RemoveCurseScrollDefenseBase.Name: Remove Curse: spell scroll defense base
RemoveCurseScrollDefenseBase.Hint: Independent homebrew base for a curse cast from a scroll. Default: 7. Any numeric value is allowed.

RemoveCurseGlyphDefenseBase.Name: Remove Curse: Spell Glyph defense base
RemoveCurseGlyphDefenseBase.Hint: Independent homebrew base for a curse cast from a Glyph. Default: 10. Any numeric value is allowed.

RemoveCurseDramaticFailureMin.Name: Remove Curse outcome: Dramatic Failure starts at
RemoveCurseDramaticFailureMin.Hint: Minimum amount by which the roll must fail for a Dramatic Curse Removal Failure. Keep this one point above the ordinary failure maximum; gaps or overlaps are normalized at resolution. Default: 5.

RemoveCurseFailureMax.Name: Remove Curse outcome: Failed Removal ends at
RemoveCurseFailureMax.Hint: Largest failure margin classified as a Failed Curse Removal. Keep this one point below the dramatic failure minimum; gaps or overlaps are normalized at resolution. Default: 4.

RemoveCurseBarelySuccessMax.Name: Remove Curse outcome: Barely Successful ends at
RemoveCurseBarelySuccessMax.Hint: Largest success margin classified as a Barely Successful Curse Removal. Keep this one point below the full success minimum; gaps or overlaps are normalized at resolution. Default: 5.

RemoveCurseSuccessMin.Name: Remove Curse outcome: Successful Removal starts at
RemoveCurseSuccessMin.Hint: Minimum success margin classified as a Successful Curse Removal without negative effects. Keep this one point above the barely successful maximum; gaps or overlaps are normalized at resolution. Default: 6.

RemoveCurseNames.Name: Recognized Remove Curse names
RemoveCurseNames.Hint: Comma-separated spell names. Parenthetical suffixes are recognized automatically.

Dialog.BonusDiceHint: Optional, for example 1d4 or 1d4 + 1d8. The GM will confirm or edit this formula.
Dialog.TargetBonusDiceHint: Optional dice added to the original spell roll. Ignored for a scroll or Glyph fixed defense.
Dialog.BonusDiceGMHint: Confirm or edit the optional dice formula. Leave blank for no additional dice.
Dialog.KnowsTargetSpell: I know the spell being countered
Dialog.KnowsTargetSpellHint: Homebrew only: a known normal spell grants advantage (except Counterspell); a known scroll or Glyph spell reduces its fixed defense by 5. The GM will confirm this declaration.
Dialog.GMConfirmKnowledge: The Counterspell caster knows the target spell
Dialog.GMConfirmKnowledgeHint: Confirm or correct the declaration. A known normal spell grants advantage except against Counterspell; a known scroll or Glyph spell instead reduces its defense by 5.
Dialog.SpecialSpellcasterCounterspell: Counterspell caster is a Special Spellcaster
Dialog.SpecialSpellcasterHint: Uses a minimum kept d20 result of {minimum}, read from the module's world settings.
Dialog.DisadvantageHint: For example, from an exhaustion level or another effect. The GM will confirm this declaration.
Dialog.GMTargetDisadvantageHint: For example, from exhaustion or another effect. This option is ignored for a spell scroll or glyph.
Dialog.GMDisadvantageHint: Confirm or correct the declaration. Advantage and disadvantage cancel each other.
Dialog.GMWillReview: The active GM will receive and review all selected values before the rolls.
Dialog.TargetPlayerTitle: Counterspell PLUS — your spell is being countered
Dialog.SpecialTargetHint: Choose an actor or token, or use Unknown/Glyph when no character sheet represents the target. Glyph automatically sets the casting source to Glyph.
Dialog.AskOwner: Ask the active owner of this actor
Dialog.AskOwnerHint: If an active player owns the selected actor, they will receive their own data window. Blind GM Roll keeps the data with the GM and suppresses that player window.
Dialog.ScrollHint: Creator values are used only when Casting source is Spell scroll or Glyph.
Dialog.ManualDataHint: These values are used for a Scroll or Glyph creator and as manual caster values when the target is Unknown. A selected actor's sheet values take precedence for a normal spell.
Dialog.PlayerDataHint: Your modifier and proficiency bonus will be read from this character sheet. The GM will review the result.
Dialog.GMReviewHint: Confirm or correct the target spell data. The Counterspell slot is consumed only after you press Roll.

Chat.CounterspellRollAdvantage: Counterspell: {actor} — advantage for knowing the target spell
Chat.AutomaticSuccess: The Counterspell slot is at least as high as the target spell. The spell is countered automatically.
Chat.HiddenResults: At least one side used a hidden roll. The outcome is public, but the hidden numerical results remain visible only according to their selected roll modes.
Chat.TieRule: In the opposed-roll rule, a tie favors the original spell.
Chat.WildMagicBody: Counterspelling another Counterspell triggers a Wild Magic effect. The GM determines the effect.

Notifications.NoSlots: This actor has no available spell slot capable of casting Counterspell.
Notifications.NoCasters: No actors or tokens are available for the GM to select.
Notifications.CasterNotFound: The selected caster could not be found.
Notifications.ActorNotFound: The Counterspell actor could not be found.
Notifications.SlotUnavailable: The selected Counterspell slot is no longer available.
Notifications.NoGM: An active GM is required to resolve Counterspell.
Notifications.AlreadyPending: This actor already has a pending Counterspell workflow.
Notifications.RequestTimeout: The Counterspell request expired after three minutes.
Notifications.CancelledByGM: The GM cancelled the Counterspell workflow.
Notifications.CancelledByTarget: The target player cancelled the Counterspell workflow.
Notifications.TargetRollFailed: The target spell roll could not be completed.
Notifications.InvalidBonusFormula: An additional dice formula is invalid. Correct it before rolling.
Notifications.SelectTargetSuggestion: Select a target from the search suggestions.
Notifications.GenericError: The Counterspell workflow encountered an error.

Dialog.OfficialBonusLater: Official 2014: you will choose optional additional dice separately for each required ability check after the GM enters the effects.
Dialog.EffectCount: Number of spell effects on the target
Dialog.SetupHint: Enter the total number of effects. With more than one effect, the homebrew rule adds that total to every effect's DC.
Dialog.SpecialTargetHint: Choose an actor/token or a special target: Unknown, Glyph or Object. A Glyph target automatically forces every effect source to Glyph.
Dialog.SourceBaseHint: Normal spell, Scroll and Glyph use their own independently configurable homebrew bases. Creator values are used for Scroll and Glyph.
Dialog.KnowsEffectHint: Homebrew: subtract 5 from this individual effect's DC. The GM will confirm this declaration.
Dialog.CheckBonusDiceHint: Official 2014: this formula applies only to the separate ability check for this effect.
Dialog.AutomaticBonusIgnored: This effect is currently automatic because its level does not exceed the Dispel Magic slot; the formula is ignored unless the GM changes its level.
Dialog.HiddenLevelBonusHint: The spell level is hidden. Enter optional dice for this effect; the GM will apply them only if an ability check is required.
Dialog.PlayerEffectsTitle: Counterspell PLUS — spells affected by your Dispel Magic
Dialog.GMConfirmKnown: The Dispel Magic caster knows this spell
Dialog.SpecialSpellcaster: Dispel Magic caster is a Special Spellcaster
Dialog.GMConfirmDisadvantage: The Dispel Magic caster has disadvantage
Dialog.GMReviewHint: Confirm or correct every effect and player declaration. The Dispel Magic slot is consumed only after you press Roll.
Dialog.GMReviewTitle: Counterspell PLUS — final Dispel Magic review

Chat.HiddenResult: At least one relevant value used a hidden roll mode.
Chat.TieRule: Under the homebrew rule, the Dispel Magic result must be strictly higher than the effect DC; a tie leaves the effect in place.
Chat.ManualRemoval: The module only identifies which effects should be removed; the GM removes them manually.

Notifications.NoSlots: This actor has no available spell slot capable of casting Dispel Magic.
Notifications.NoTargets: No actors or tokens are available for the GM to select.
Notifications.TargetNotFound: The selected Dispel Magic target could not be found.
Notifications.ActorNotFound: The Dispel Magic actor could not be found.
Notifications.SlotUnavailable: The selected Dispel Magic slot is no longer available.
Notifications.NoGM: An active GM is required to resolve Dispel Magic.
Notifications.AlreadyPending: This actor already has a pending Dispel Magic workflow.
Notifications.RequestTimeout: The Dispel Magic request expired after three minutes.
Notifications.DefensePostFailed: The GM could not publish the Dispel Magic passive defenses.
Notifications.CancelledByGM: The GM cancelled the Dispel Magic workflow.
Notifications.CancelledByPlayer: The Dispel Magic caster cancelled the workflow.
Notifications.GenericError: The Dispel Magic workflow encountered an error.

Dialog.OfficialBonusLater: Official 2014: you will choose optional additional dice separately for each required ability check after the GM enters the curses.
Dialog.EffectCount: Number of curses on the target
Dialog.SetupHint: Enter the total number of curses. With more than one curse, the homebrew rule adds that total to every curse's DC.
Dialog.SpecialTargetHint: Choose an actor/token or a special target: Unknown, Glyph or Object. A Glyph target automatically forces every curse source to Glyph.
Dialog.SourceBaseHint: Normal spell, Scroll and Glyph use their own independently configurable homebrew bases. Creator values are used for Scroll and Glyph.
Dialog.KnowsEffectHint: Homebrew: subtract 5 from this individual curse's DC. The GM will confirm this declaration.
Dialog.CheckBonusDiceHint: Official 2014: this formula applies only to the separate ability check for this curse.
Dialog.AutomaticBonusIgnored: This curse is currently automatic because its level does not exceed the Remove Curse slot; the formula is ignored unless the GM changes its level.
Dialog.HiddenLevelBonusHint: The curse level is hidden. Enter optional dice for this curse; the GM will apply them only if an ability check is required.
Dialog.PlayerEffectsTitle: Counterspell PLUS — curses affected by your Remove Curse
Dialog.GMConfirmKnown: The Remove Curse caster knows this curse
Dialog.SpecialSpellcaster: Remove Curse caster is a Special Spellcaster
Dialog.GMConfirmDisadvantage: The Remove Curse caster has disadvantage
Dialog.GMReviewHint: Confirm or correct every curse and player declaration. The Remove Curse slot is consumed only after you press Roll.
Dialog.GMReviewTitle: Counterspell PLUS — final Remove Curse review

Chat.HiddenResult: At least one relevant value used a hidden roll mode.
Chat.TieRule: Under the homebrew rule, the Remove Curse result must be strictly higher than the curse DC; a tie is a failure.
Chat.ManualRemoval: The module only identifies which curses should be removed; the GM removes them manually.

DramaticFailure.Title: Dramatic Curse Removal Failure
DramaticFailure.Body: Remove Curse failed by {minimum} or more. This carries significant complications. The GM determines the effect.

Failure.Title: Failed Curse Removal
Failure.Body: Remove Curse failed by {minimum}–{maximum}. This carries minor complications. The GM determines the effect.

BarelySuccess.Title: Barely Successful Curse Removal
BarelySuccess.Body: Remove Curse succeeded by {minimum}–{maximum}. The curse is removed, but this carries minor complications. The GM determines the effect.

Success.Title: Successful Curse Removal
Success.Body: Remove Curse succeeded by {minimum} or more. The curse is removed without negative effects.

Notifications.NoSlots: This actor has no available spell slot capable of casting Remove Curse.
Notifications.NoTargets: No actors or tokens are available for the GM to select.
Notifications.TargetNotFound: The selected Remove Curse target could not be found.
Notifications.ActorNotFound: The Remove Curse actor could not be found.
Notifications.SlotUnavailable: The selected Remove Curse slot is no longer available.
Notifications.NoGM: An active GM is required to resolve Remove Curse.
Notifications.AlreadyPending: This actor already has a pending Remove Curse workflow.
Notifications.RequestTimeout: The Remove Curse request expired after three minutes.
Notifications.DefensePostFailed: The GM could not publish the Remove Curse passive defenses.
Notifications.CancelledByGM: The GM cancelled the Remove Curse workflow.
Notifications.CancelledByPlayer: The Remove Curse caster cancelled the workflow.
Notifications.GenericError: The Remove Curse workflow encountered an error.
